# AISearch Global — Full Reference
# Last updated: 9 Jul 2026
https://aisearch.global

> AISearch Global is a specialist Answer Engine Optimisation (AEO) agency based in Sydney, Australia. We help Australian businesses get found, cited, and recommended inside AI-generated answers on ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews, DeepSeek, and Grok.

---

## About AISearch Global

AISearch Global is a pure-play AEO specialist. Every other Australian digital agency treats AEO as an add-on to SEO. AISearch Global focuses on it exclusively. Founded by a Sydney-based AEO strategist with approximately 50 certifications across AI, generative AI, AI ethics, psychology, human-centred AI, and leadership.

**Stage:** Active. Launched May 2026.
**Location:** Sydney, New South Wales, Australia
**Contact:** hello@aisearch.global
**Website:** https://aisearch.global

---

## Services

### AI Visibility Audit — $1,500–$2,500 AUD
URL: /services/aeo-audit

The primary offer. A comprehensive baseline audit of how AI engines currently find, describe, and recommend a business. Includes a full audit of 13 AEO signals across technical structure, content, schema markup, and entity signals. Delivers a prioritised 90-day action plan with specific fixes ranked by expected visibility impact.

Also includes a third-party citation gap analysis — six checks covering: citation inventory (where the brand is currently mentioned across industry publications, news, directories, review platforms, podcasts, and expert roundups); competitor citation gap (the 3 competitors most cited by AI engines for the target queries, and which sources cite them but not you); source authority mapping (target prompts tested across ChatGPT, Gemini, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews, and Claude, with cited sources traced back to origin); review signal health (volume, recency, and rating consistency across the platforms AI engines pull from); entity corroboration (whether third-party sources confirm the same name, location, services, and credentials the website claims); and Wikipedia/Wikidata eligibility. Delivered as a ranked citation-gap list — the 10 sources most likely to move AI visibility, ordered by impact and achievability, each with the concrete action required.

### Content Restructuring — from $1,500 AUD
URL: /services/content-restructuring

Converts existing website copy into answer-ready formats optimised for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems and LLM extraction. Focuses on question-and-answer formatting, definition blocks, direct-answer paragraphs, and structured headings that AI engines can parse and cite.

### Schema Implementation — $750–$1,500 AUD
URL: /services/schema-implementation

Deploys structured JSON-LD markup including Organization, LocalBusiness, FAQPage, Article, BreadcrumbList, and Service schema types. Strengthens semantic trust and machine comprehension of business identity, services, and expertise.

### AI Brand Presence — $1,200–$1,800 AUD
URL: /services/ai-brand-presence

Builds consistent entity signals across the data sources AI engines use as grounding references — Wikipedia, Wikidata, industry directories, authoritative citations, and structured third-party mentions. Ensures AI engines correctly identify, describe, and attribute the business.

### Structured Content Systems — $1,500–$2,500 AUD
URL: /services/structured-content-systems

Creates reusable publishing frameworks that produce machine-readable, high-utility pages at scale. Includes content templates, FAQ systems, glossary structures, and answer-format page designs that compound AI visibility over time.

### AI Visibility Strategy — $2,000–$3,500 AUD
URL: /services/ai-visibility-strategy

Aligns an AEO optimisation roadmap with specific business revenue targets and priority market queries. Combines technical AEO, content strategy, and entity building into a cohesive plan with measurable milestones.

---

## AEO Tools & Resources

URL: https://aisearch.global/resources/
Type: CollectionPage

AISearch Global provides three free resources to help Australian businesses understand and improve their AI visibility:

1. AEO Score Calculator — free interactive tool at /aeo-score-calculator. Assesses AI visibility across entity clarity, schema, content structure, and citation authority.
2. AEO Glossary — free reference at /insights/aeo-traction-stack#aeo-glossary. Plain-language definitions for AEO, GEO, schema, entity signals, and citation authority terms.
3. The AI Search Dictionary: AEO, GEO & AI Visibility Terms Explained in Plain English — 368 key terms by Viveka Mohan Das, available on Amazon at https://a.co/d/0fvTsI4V (ASIN B0H69QZ9RZ). Covers AEO, GEO, AI visibility, entity optimisation, structured data, prompt testing, and citation share in plain language. Includes bonus 80/20 checklist and action templates for small business owners.

---

## Free Diagnostic Tool

### AISearch Global AEO Score Calculator
URL: /aeo-score-calculator

A free, instant AI visibility scorecard. Enter a website URL, industry, and location to receive:
- A numeric score out of 100
- A letter grade (A to F) with label
- An AI recommendation likelihood score (%)
- A mention probability score (%)
- A plain-English interpretation of the score
- The single highest-impact fix with estimated point gain
- An industry benchmark comparison (average and top performer)

**20 signals scored — 17 structural AEO + 3 Princeton GEO:**
1. Title tag present and descriptive
2. Meta description present
3. H1 tag present
4. Content depth (word count)
5. JSON-LD schema markup
6. FAQ section present
7. About page linked
8. Contact signals present
9. Review / testimonial signals
10. Location signals
11. Industry keyword relevance
12. Sitemap.xml present
13. Robots.txt present
14. Internal link density
15. Trust page count
16. Structured headings (H2/H3)
17. Answer-format content signals
18. Statistics present (Princeton GEO signal)
19. Source citations present (Princeton GEO signal)
20. Quotations present (Princeton GEO signal)

Signals 18–20 are based on the peer-reviewed Princeton/Georgia Tech KDD 2024 GEO study, which found these three tactics boost AI citation rates by up to 40%.

Scoring engine runs on Cloudflare Workers. No email required. Results are instant.

---

## Proprietary Framework: The AEO Traction Stack

Four layers, in order of implementation priority:

1. **Entity Clarity** — Ensure AI engines can correctly identify who you are, what you do, where you operate, and who you serve. Requires consistent NAP data, clear business description, and structured identity signals.
2. **Schema Markup** — Deploy JSON-LD structured data. Organization, LocalBusiness, FAQPage, Article, and Service schemas are the highest-impact types for most Australian businesses.
3. **Answer-Format Content** — Rewrite key pages in direct-answer format. Leads with the answer, uses question-based headings, includes definition blocks and FAQ sections. Makes content extractable by RAG systems.
4. **Citation Consistency** — Build authoritative third-party mentions. Directories, press, industry bodies, and backlinks that corroborate your entity signals across the web.

Layers 1 and 2 account for approximately 80% of achievable improvement for most businesses starting from zero.

---

## Published Insights

### What Is AEO? Answer Engine Optimisation Explained
URL: /insights/what-is-aeo-answer-engine-optimisation
Published: 24 May 2026

Foundational guide explaining what Answer Engine Optimisation is, how it differs from traditional SEO, which AI platforms are covered (ChatGPT, Gemini, Perplexity, Claude, Google AI Overviews, DeepSeek, Grok), and the core signals that drive AI search visibility. Written for Australian business owners with no prior AEO knowledge.

### How AI Decides Which Businesses to Recommend
URL: /insights/how-ai-decides-which-businesses-to-recommend
Published: 30 May 2026

Explains the signals, data sources, and trust factors AI engines use when selecting businesses to recommend in a generated answer. Covers entity recognition, schema markup, content extractability, citation authority, and recency signals. Includes practical implications for Australian SMBs.

### The AEO Traction Stack
URL: /insights/aeo-traction-stack
Published: 7 Jun 2026

Full explanation of AISearch Global's proprietary four-layer AEO framework. Covers each layer in detail — Entity Clarity, Schema Markup, Answer-Format Content, Citation Consistency — with implementation guidance, common mistakes, and expected impact at each layer. Includes a self-assessment checklist.

### The Princeton GEO Paper Explained for Australian Business Owners
URL: /insights/princeton-geo-paper-explained
Published: 14 Jun 2026

Plain-English breakdown of the peer-reviewed Princeton/Georgia Tech study on Generative Engine Optimisation (KDD 2024). Tested 9 content tactics across 10,000 real AI queries. Three tactics — cite sources, add statistics, add quotations — boosted AI citation rates by up to 40%. Keyword stuffing actively reduced AI visibility. Covers practical implications for Australian business owners seeking AI search citations.

### AISearch Global: Client Zero — From 19 to 91, and What AI Still Doesn't Know
URL: /insights/aisearch-global-client-zero
Published: 21 Jun 2026 | Last modified: 24 Jun 2026

AISearch Global used its own brand as Client Zero — running a full AI Visibility Audit on itself from launch on 20 May 2026. Documents a structural AEO score climb from 19 to 91 in six weeks. Records what ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Gemini actually say about the brand: Brand Recognition 10/100, Share of Voice 11.7%, Brand Sentiment 56/100, GEO Visibility 45/100, overall AEO score 36/100 average. Explains the AI training cutoff gap (models scored the brand before its data reached training sets) and what to do about it. Accompanied by the interactive Client Zero AI Visibility Console.

### What Happens When Customers Ask AI a Question
URL: /insights/what-happens-when-customers-ask-ai
Published: 19 Jul 2026

A practical guide for Australian small business owners on how customer search is shifting from keyword search (SEO) to AI answers (AEO). Uses cat toothpaste (search interest up roughly 230% over two years, ~12,100 monthly searches) to show how people now ask AI full natural questions instead of typing keywords into Google. Walks through what actually happens in the seconds after a customer asks: the question is broken into tokens, the AI checks whether it needs a live web search (triggered by words like "best", "near me", "top-rated", "compare"), the answer is built one token at a time, and a business is named only if it was findable, readable, and trustworthy-looking. Contrasts the two models: SEO returns a ranked list of 50 to 100 links the customer must compare, while an AI tool writes one direct, summarised answer with room to name only one or two businesses. Explains decision fatigue as the reason customers gravitate toward a single clear answer, and why a business can rank on Google yet never be named by AI (ranking and being citable are two different skills). Stresses that AEO is not just SEO with a new label. Gives six plain-English steps that make AI more likely to recommend a business: update your Google Business Profile, pass the one-sentence test (plain English), encourage specific reviews, keep name/address/phone/details consistent everywhere, describe everyday value in simple terms, and do the 30-second check (ask ChatGPT or Gemini a real customer question and see if you are named). Core message: the clarity that helps AI understand you is the same clarity that helps human customers trust you, and AEO adds a layer on top of SEO rather than replacing it.

---

## Interactive Tools

### Client Zero AI Visibility Console
URL: /insights/client-zero-visibility-dashboard
Last updated: 25 Jun 2026

Interactive dashboard presenting AISearch Global's complete AI Visibility Audit across 11 scored metrics. Scored independently by ChatGPT (GPT-5.4 mini), Perplexity (real-time), and Gemini 3 Flash Preview. Metrics: AEO Score (36/100 avg), Brand Recognition (10/100), Share of Voice (11.7% avg), Brand Sentiment (56/100), Presence Quality (4.3/20), GEO Visibility (45/100), Narrative Themes, Source Analysis, Site Performance (98/100 PageSpeed), Technical Foundation (5.6/10 Cloudflare health), and Analysis Summary. Data graded 20 Jun 2026. Designed as a living case study and product demonstration of the AI Visibility Audit service.

---

## The Answer Engine — Daily AI News

### The Answer Engine
URL: /news

The Answer Engine is AISearch Global's daily AI news publication, presented in a broadsheet-newspaper format. Each edition covers four beats: AI news, AI law and regulation, human-AI research, and AEO-relevant developments, with an Australian focus. A new edition is published every day by an automated pipeline; each edition is archived permanently at a dated clean URL (/news/YYYY-MM-DD). Every story links back to its original source — AISearch Global rounds up, it does not republish. The hub page carries a live ticker of the latest headlines plus the full archive list. RSS feed at /news/rss.xml. First edition published 5 July 2026.

---

## Reference Pages

### FAQ — 51 AEO Questions Answered
URL: /faq

51 frequently asked questions covering: what AEO is and how it differs from SEO, how AI engines decide which businesses to recommend, what schema markup does and how to implement it, what the AI Visibility Audit includes, how long AEO takes to show results, what industries benefit most, and how to work with AISearch Global.

### AI Visibility Audit
URL: /#contact

Homepage contact form for businesses exploring an AI Visibility Audit. Collects website URL, industry, business description, current pain points, and contact details. No obligation. Used to assess fit and prepare a relevant audit scope.

### About AISearch Global
URL: /about/

Background on the agency, the founder, the AEO-only focus, and the approach to client work. Includes founder credentials: ~50 certifications across AI, generative AI, AI ethics, psychology, mental health, human-centred AI, and leadership.

### Privacy Policy
URL: /privacy/

URL: /terms/

## Schema Implemented

- `Organization` — home page
- `ProfessionalService` — home page
- `WebSite` — home page
- `FAQPage` — home page, /faq, and all 6 service pages
- `Article` — all 5 insights articles
- `BreadcrumbList` — all 5 insights articles and all 6 service pages
- `Service` — all 6 service pages
- `HowTo` — /insights/aeo-traction-stack (4-step implementation guide)
- `WebPage` with `speakable` — all 5 insights articles
- `CollectionPage` — /insights/ and /news (news archive with ItemList of dated editions)
- `NewsArticle` — every dated daily edition page (/news/YYYY-MM-DD)
- `Periodical` — "The Answer Engine" (isPartOf on every NewsArticle; hasPart on the /news CollectionPage)
- `ItemList` — /insights/ (article list)
- `BreadcrumbList` — /faq (site-wide via JSON-LD)

---

## Technical

- Last refreshed: 8 Jul 2026 (daily AI news publication at /news named The Answer Engine — broadsheet redesign, Periodical schema, RSS feed at /news/rss.xml, and "The Answer Engine" in the main navigation)
- sitemap.xml: https://aisearch.global/sitemap.xml
- robots.txt: https://aisearch.global/robots.txt
- llms.txt: https://aisearch.global/llms.txt
- Hosted on Cloudflare Pages, deployed from GitHub on push to main
- AEO Score Calculator scoring: Cloudflare Workers (aeo-score.aisearchglobal.workers.dev)
